title: streblus crenatus
intro: Streblus crenatus, also known as the Koki tree or Monkey tree, is a species of fig tree native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Streblus crenatus are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Streblus crenatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional remedies to promote overall well-being and immunity.
